name: Dead Bug
resistance_profile: Anti-extension: the abs isometrically keep the lower back pinned to the floor while the opposite arm and leg lower and lengthen the lever.
cues: Lower back glued to the floor the whole time
Move the opposite arm and leg together, slowly
Exhale and brace as the limbs lower
execution_steps: Lie on your back with arms reaching straight up and hips and knees bent to 90 degrees.
Press your lower back flat into the floor by bracing the abs and tucking the pelvis.
Slowly lower one arm overhead and the opposite leg toward the floor, keeping the back flat.
Return to the start and repeat with the other arm and leg, alternating.
good_when: You're learning to brace and keep a neutral spine
You want a lower-back-friendly beginner core exercise
bad_when: You need heavy loading for ab hypertrophy
You can't yet keep the lower back flat even with limbs bent
common_errors: Letting the lower back arch off the floor
Rushing the reps or holding the breath
progressions: Lower the limbs closer to the floor with a slower tempo
Add a light band or ankle weights
regressions: Move only the legs, or only the arms
Reduce how far the limbs lower
